methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Tempus AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $6B+

private.name: Freenome
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $2.6B

private.name: Alto Neuroscience
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Recursion Pharmaceuticals, a public-stage biotech company founded by Chris Gibson and Dean Li, is evaluated against a peer universe of 3 same-sector public comparables in Biotech & Health, supplemented by 8 private frontier-tech firms including Tempus AI, Freenome, and Alto Neuroscience.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, drawing from three public-stage Biotech & Health peers. Tempus AI trades at $6.0B, Freenome at $2.6B, and Alto Neuroscience at $529M, yielding a median valuation of $2.6B and a range spanning $529M to $6.0B. A public-comp anchoring approach using P/S multiples (median 6.6x across the same three tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue data, typical for private or newly public firms. The target's current valuation of $1.8B sits below the peer median, reflecting either discount positioning or stage-specific risk factors not resolved in available data.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $2.2B (low) to $3.0B (high), centered on $2.6B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($529M–$6.0B), the limited sample size for statistical robustness, and lack of revenue or pipeline maturity data to differentiate Recursion's competitive positioning within the biotech cohort.
